_comment: KHY OS bootable kernel ISO download manifest. The wheel ships this file but NEVER the ISO (MANIFEST.in prunes *.iso). The ISO is platform-independent (one GRUB image boots on any x86_64 host via QEMU), so a single url/sha256 serves every platform. Until a release ISO is published, url/sha256 stay empty and ensureKhyosIso() falls through to the local kernel/build ISO or an actionable 'build locally' error. To publish: build with `make -C kernel iso`, upload khy-os-kernel.iso, then fill url + the `sha256sum` of that file here. KHY_KHYOS_MIRROR_BASE overrides the host; KHY_KERNEL_ISO overrides everything with a local path.
_comment_build_assets: builderAppliance + qemu power WSL/Docker-free native-Windows builds: ensureBuilderAppliance() / ensurePortableQemu() fetch them sha256-pinned on demand and cache under ~/.khyos/cache/{builder,qemu} (legacy ~/.khyquant/khyos still read on existing machines). They are runtime downloads, never bundled in the wheel. Leave url/sha256 empty to keep the rung inert (the build cascade then degrades to downloading the prebuilt ISO, then a guide). qemu keys are `${process.platform}-${process.arch}`; systemBinRelPath/imgBinRelPath are paths INSIDE the extracted archive.
_comment_toolchain: toolchain powers the PREFERRED native-Windows build (no Docker, no WSL, no VM): ensureWindowsBuildToolchain() fetches clang+ld.lld (llvm), nasm, limine, xorriso, make, and busybox sha256-pinned on demand and caches them under ~/.khyos/cache/toolchain/<platform>-<arch> (legacy ~/.khyquant/khyos/toolchain still read). All-or-nothing: every tool must be pinned (url+sha256) or the whole native rung degrades. *RelPath are paths INSIDE each extracted archive; single-file tools (busybox) set archive:false. RESILIENCE: each tool may carry an optional `mirrors:[...]` array of BYTE-IDENTICAL fallback URLs (same sha256); the downloader retries transient failures with backoff and fails over across primary -> mirrors so one dead/blocked host no longer sinks the build. KHY_KHYOS_MIRROR_BASE rehosts every tool under one base (tried first). CN FALLBACK: every github-hosted tool (llvm/limine/xorriso) AUTOMATICALLY gains ghproxy-mirrored fallback URLs (byte-identical, same sha256) so a blocked github.com no longer sinks the build on CN networks -- no manifest edit needed. Tune via KHY_KHYOS_CN_MIRRORS=<comma-prefixes> (override the ghproxy list), KHY_KHYOS_PREFER_CN=1 (try the CN mirrors FIRST, before the direct upstream -- skips per-tool timeouts when github is known-blocked), or KHY_KHYOS_NO_CN_MIRROR=1 (disable). nasm/sourceforge/frippery are not on github, so they still rely on a proxy (HTTPS_PROXY) or KHY_KHYOS_MIRROR_BASE. STABILITY: do NOT pin a GitHub branch archive (/archive/refs/heads/<branch>.zip) -- its sha drifts when the branch moves. Re-pin to an immutable commit URL with `node scripts/release/pin-khyos-toolchain.js` (resolves branch->commit, recomputes sha; `--check` fails CI on drift or any remaining branch archive). Pin the limine binary release to match kernel/iso/boot/limine/limine.conf syntax.

limine._note: Binary-release asset (prebuilt limine.exe host tool + limine-bios*.bin / limine-uefi-cd.bin boot blobs), NOT the source tarball. dirRelPath holds the boot blobs the iso-limine target copies; the Windows host tool sits one level deeper under limine-tool-windows-x86/.

xorriso._note: PeyTy cygwin build: xorriso.exe needs cygwin1.dll + cygiconv-2.dll, which ship in the same archive dir and Windows loads next to the exe.

make._note: SourceForge's downloads.sourceforge.net often serves an HTML interstitial instead of the file; pin a direct mirror host (*.dl.sourceforge.net) as primary with byte-identical mirror failover (same sha256).
